text:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: SEV-2 declared by @Sean Gallagher
Service: Hosted API (routing + gateway)
Impact: Elevated request failures + QPS drop in region ap-southeast-1
Start: 1740123301
Primary symptom: traffic dropped ~28-35% in ap-southeast-1 within 10m of rollout

Sean Gallagher: Seeing a sharp QPS cliff in ap-southeast-1 starting ~10 mins after we enabled `residency_enforcement_enabled` canary to 25% there. Error rate is up but not huge globally; regional looks bad. Can someone confirm if we’re blocking requests with the new residency contract?

Allison Grant: Pulling dashboards. ap-southeast-1 `gateway.http_requests_total` down ~32%. `gateway.responses{status=409}` up from near-zero to ~3.1% in ap-southeast-1. Also a bump in 503s from router (~1.8%). Not seeing same in us-east-1/eu-west-1.

Rafael Mendes: On-call check: customers in APAC complaining? Any Support pings yet?...

text:
incident-bot: [INC-2025-0217] Triggered: rollout-canary stuck provisioning in us-east. Suspected quota exhaustion.
Grace OConnor: anyone from Platform around? console rollout canary says it needs 2 more GPUs but provision keeps failing
Avery Johnson: looking. seeing cloud errors: quota exceeded on A100 in us-east for canary pool
Ethan Park: customer impact?
Avery Johnson: yes, canary policy paused; rollout blocked and fallback pinned longer than expected. latency creeping for a subset
Rohan Mehta: I can file quota bump but might take 30-60m. can we switch canary to us-west?
Avery Johnson: yep. switching region + lowering canary size. should unblock
Grace OConnor: logging as capacity/quota incident. pls open INT ticket + link here
Avery Johnson: created INT-489. will add details.
incident-bot: Status update: mitigated. rollout unblocked; monitoring.

text:
Incident Bot: P0 declared: Elevated 5xx + latency in us-east-1 for Hosted API. Incident channel created: #inc-2025-02-19-hosted-api-us-east

Grace O'Connor: I'm IC. Hannah ops lead. Ava comms. Dev infra. Alex gateway.

Hannah Schmitt: seeing gateway_5xx_rate us-east-1 ~28% on /v1/chat.completions. runtime queue depth rising. us-west-2 looks clean. proposing regional failover to us-west-2.

Dev Patel: +1. I can execute trafficctl. Will do weighted 90/10 then 50/50 then 0/100.

Grace O'Connor: approve. This is an emergency change; capture commands here. Need 2nd sign-off.

Alex Martinez: 2nd sign-off: approved. Let's do it.

Dev Patel: setting TTL 60

```
trafficctl dns set-ttl --record api.redwoodinference.com --ttl 60
```

Dev Patel: shifting 10% to us-west-2
```
trafficctl gslb set-weights --record api.redwoodinference.com --weights us-east-1-gw=90,us-west-2-gw=10
```

Hannah Schmitt...

title: #incidents: 1739440200
preview:
incident-bot: :warning: Near-miss reported during Private upgrade window (customer env). No customer-facing outage declared, but rollback was considered. Please add notes/action items here so we can feed runbook + precheck updates.

Rafael Mendes: Sharing quick context. This was the Cascade VPC private env upgrade a...
text:
incident-bot: :warning: Near-miss reported during Private upgrade window (customer env). No customer-facing outage declared, but rollback was considered. Please add notes/action items here so we can feed runbook + precheck updates.

Rafael Mendes: Sharing quick context. This was the Cascade VPC private env upgrade attempt from 2025.02.1 -> 2025.03.0. Upgrade started fine, then DB migration job hung ~18 min and we saw request error rate spike in control-plane API (5xx). Customer paused traffic + we stopped before proceeding to data-plane.

Sean Gallagher: Confirming SRE view: we stayed within SLO because traffic was low and customer had a maintenance window, but this was one step away from a real incident. The scary part was we didn’t have a deterministic “stop here / rollback now” point communicated to operator.

Connor O'Brien: We need to capture *exact* failure mode. Was it migratio...

text:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: PagerDuty alert fired PD-INC-4721 — canary error rate spike after rollout to gp-us-west-2-1. Metric: canary_http_errors(percent_5m) = 9.2% (threshold 5%). Release: rtx-service v3.4.1-canary. Link: https://pagerduty.example/INC4721

pagerduty: Page sent to on-call Priya Chen (SRE primary). Escalation policy: realtime->sre->eng-runtime.

Priya Chen: ACK. I'm on it. Can anyone confirm if this is purely canary traffic or beginning to bleed into prod?

Ethan Park: preliminary look — errors are 502/504 from the runtime sidecar in canaries. Traffic still ~95% to prod main; canary is ~5%. I see an N+1 K/V cache eviction pattern around 10:02 UTC.

Maya Singh: seeing similar in logs: repeated redis timeouts in the canary bucket. Sample stack: `LLMRequest -> runtime-proxy -> kv-cache -> redis` — kv-cache misses spiked.

title: #incidents: 1719001111
preview:
Alice Nguyen: FYI we've had 3 DB connection incidents in ~6 weeks. Before we finalize postmortem actions, let's decide customer comms + RCA sharing rules. Quick thread.

Ben Carter: Agreed. Two asks from customers last time: clearer statuspage wording and an easy way to request full RCAs.

Priya Shah: Pattern looks ...
text:
Alice Nguyen: FYI we've had 3 DB connection incidents in ~6 weeks. Before we finalize postmortem actions, let's decide customer comms + RCA sharing rules. Quick thread.

Ben Carter: Agreed. Two asks from customers last time: clearer statuspage wording and an easy way to request full RCAs.

Priya Shah: Pattern looks consistent with batch-window spikes. I'll attach traces to the postmortem doc. For comms: short public line + offer to provide a redacted RCA on request.

Marco Ruiz: Recommendation for statuspage phrasing: "Intermittent database connectivity impacted a subset of inference requests; mitigations are in place and we are monitoring." Keeps it specific but calm.

Elena Park: Security/Legal: ok with that blurb. Policy: default to redacted RCAs for public/customer sharing. Full unredacted RCAs require explicit customer consent recorded via Support and Legal sign-off.

Alice Nguye...
